assumptions: The intragroup dividend receivable is a monetary item denominated in a currency different from at least one entity's functional currency.
The question concerns consolidated financial statements in which the related foreign exchange differences could affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge of foreign currency risk
approaches.rationale: A recognised asset can be designated in a fair value hedge for a particular risk, and the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary receivable may qualify in consolidation if the exchange differences are not fully eliminated.
conditions: the dividend receivable is a recognised monetary asset
the counterparties have different functional currencies
the foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation
only the foreign currency risk component is designated
the IFRS 9 designation, documentation and effectiveness requirements are met

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge of foreign currency risk
approaches.rationale: A recognised asset can be hedged for variability in cash flows attributable to a particular risk, so the foreign currency component of the intragroup receivable may be documented this way if it affects consolidated profit or loss.
conditions: the intragroup item qualifies under the foreign-currency exception for intragroup monetary items
the foreign currency risk affects consolidated profit or loss
only the foreign currency risk component is designated
the IFRS 9 designation, documentation and effectiveness requirements are met

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale: This hedge model applies only to foreign currency risk on a net investment in a foreign operation, not to an intragroup dividend receivable as such.
conditions: it becomes relevant only if the hedged item is the net assets of a foreign operation rather than the dividend receivable

approaches.label: No hedge accounting in consolidation
approaches.rationale: Intragroup items are generally not eligible hedged items in consolidated financial statements unless the specific foreign currency exceptions are met.
conditions: the item is not an eligible intragroup monetary item
or the foreign currency risk does not affect consolidated profit or loss
or the IFRS 9 documentation and effectiveness criteria are not satisfied
